title: Code of Hammurabi
context: One of the earliest written legal codes — 282 laws carved in stone so no ruler could claim ignorance of justice.

title: Cyrus Cylinder
context: Cyrus the Great's declaration upon conquering Babylon — religious tolerance, freedom from bondage, and restoration of temples. Sometimes called the first charter of human rights.

title: Behistun Inscription
context: Darius I's trilingual proclamation carved 100 meters up a sheer cliff — the 'Rosetta Stone of cuneiform' that unlocked Mesopotamian languages.

title: Rosetta Stone
context: Decree honoring Ptolemy V, inscribed in three scripts — the key that unlocked Egyptian hieroglyphs.

title: Magna Carta
context: Charter of rights forced upon King John — the foundation of constitutional law and individual liberty.

title: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
context: Adopted by the UN General Assembly — the most translated document in human history, existing in 500+ languages.

title: Pioneer Plaque
context: Attached to Pioneer 10 — a message to extraterrestrials using no human language, only universal symbols.

title: Arecibo Message
context: Three-minute binary transmission beamed at star cluster M13, 25,000 light-years away — a message designed never to be received, aimed at proving we could send one.

title: Voyager Golden Record
context: Kurt Waldheim's greeting aboard Voyagers 1 and 2 — now the most distant human-made objects in existence.

title: Georgia Guidestones
context: Anonymously commissioned guidelines for rebuilding civilization — destroyed by bombing in 2022, preserved only in text.
